Overall:
The following treatment processes are considered effective for the removal of nitrobenzene: adsorptive media using MCM-41 (up to 72 percent removal) 1987, biological filtration (up to 98 percent removal)1913, biological treatment using Shewanella putrefaciens CN32 1900, Comamonas sp. Strain JS765 (up to 100 percent removal) 1909, ozone in combination of copper and Fe(III) (95 percent removal) 1923, ultraviolet irradiation (up to 90 percent) 1921, ultraviolet irradiation hydrogen peroxide (up to 100 percent) 1920,  and ultraviolet irradiation ozone (up to 100 percent) 1922, 1923.  Performance of these technologies can vary based on water quality and treatment characteristics.
